perturbation.original_text: In order to maintain the exclusivity granted hereunder, Zanotti shall provide Aura with orders for a minimum of (i) one thousand (1,000) AETRU Systems during the first twenty-four (24) months of this Agreement and (ii) seven hundred and fifty (750) AETRU Systems per year thereafter for so long as this Agreement remains in effect (the "Minimum Order"). In the event that Zanotti fails to secure purchases amounting to the Minimum Order for any particular period, the exclusive supplier rights granted pursuant to this Article 2 shall become non- exclusive commencing immediately following such period in which the Minimum Order was not achieved and Aura shall have full discretion to purchase or otherwise obtain Product from sources other than Zanotti.
perturbation.changed_text: In order to maintain the exclusivity granted hereunder, Zanotti shall provide Aura with orders for a minimum of (i) one thousand (1,000) AETRU Systems during the first twenty-four (24) months of this Agreement and (ii) seven hundred and fifty (750) AETRU Systems per year thereafter for so long as this Agreement remains in effect (the "Minimum Order"). In the event that Zanotti fails to secure purchases amounting to the Minimum Order for any particular period, the exclusive supplier rights granted pursuant to this Article 2 shall become non- exclusive commencing immediately following such period in which the Minimum Order was not achieved and Aura shall have full discretion to purchase or otherwise obtain Product from sources other than Zanotti. However, Zanotti shall maintain exclusive supplier rights if Zanotti pays a penalty of 10% of the shortfall amount.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction by adding a clause that allows Zanotti to maintain exclusive supplier rights by paying a penalty, even if they fail to meet the minimum order requirements. This directly contradicts the original clause, which states that failure to meet the minimum order automatically results in the loss of exclusivity, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appointment. Pursuant to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, Aura appoints Zanotti as its exclusive supplier of the Products within the Territory and Field of Use and Zanotti hereby accepts such appointment. In order to maintain the exclusivity granted hereunder, Zanotti shall provide Aura with orders for a minimum of (i) one thousand (1,000) AETRU Systems during the first twenty-four (24) months of this Agreement and (ii) seven hundred and fifty (750) AETRU Systems per year thereafter for so long as this Agreement remains in effect (the "Minimum Order"). In the event that Zanotti fails to secure purchases amounting to the Minimum Order for any particular period, the exclusive supplier rights granted pursuant to this Article 2 shall become non- exclusive commencing immediately following such period in which the Minimum Order was not achieved and Aura shall have full discretion to purchase or otherwise obtain Product from sources other than Zanotti. However, Zanotti shall maintain exclusive supplier rights if Zanotti pays a penalty of 10% of the shortfall amount. Aura shall serve as seller for all orders for AETRU Systems (whether such orders are provided by Zanotti or otherwise) and shall directly invoice all buyers and collect the purchase price for all AETRU Systems sold.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that failing to meet the minimum order quantity automatically results in the loss of exclusivity. The modified text adds an exception, allowing Zanotti to maintain exclusivity by paying a penalty, directly contradicting the original condition for maintaining exclusivity as stated earlier in the same text. This creates an inconsistency and ambiguity regarding the conditions under which exclusivity is maintained, leading to a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Unless terminated prior to the natural expiration of the Initial Term, upon the expiry of the Initial Term this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive terms of the same duration, unless either party gives written notice to the other of such party's desire not to renew not less than ninety (90) days prior to the date of the expiration of the Initial Term or any successive term thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless terminated prior to the natural expiration of the Initial Term, upon the expiry of the Initial Term this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive terms of the same duration, unless either party gives written notice to the other of such party's desire not to renew not less than sixty (60) days prior to the date of the expiration of the Initial Term or any successive term thereafter. However, if neither party provides notice, the agreement shall only renew for a single, one-year term.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a conflict by changing the notice period for non-renewal from 90 days to 60 days and adding that the agreement will only renew for a single one-year term if no notice is given. The original text implies indefinite renewals unless notice is given, whereas the added sentence limits renewals to a single year if no notice is provided. This generates a contradiction regarding the term of renewal if no notice is provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. This Agreement shall be for a period of five (5) years commencing upon the Effective Date hereof unless sooner terminated in accordance with this Agreement (the "Initial Term"). Unless terminated prior to the natural expiration of the Initial Term, upon the expiry of the Initial Term this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive terms of the same duration, unless either party gives written notice to the other of such party's desire not to renew not less than sixty (60) days prior to the date of the expiration of the Initial Term or any successive term thereafter. However, if neither party provides notice, the agreement shall only renew for a single, one-year term. For purposes of this Agreement, "Term" shall mean collectively the Initial Term as well as any successive renewal or extension thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal terms. The original text implies automatic renewals for successive terms of the same duration unless notice is given. The changed text adds a condition that if no notice is given, the agreement renews only for a single one-year term. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the added sentence limiting renewals to a single year if no notice is provided directly contradicts the initial statement regarding successive renewals of the same duration without such a limitation. This creates a clear contradiction regarding the renewal term if no notice is provided, directly altering the obligation stated in the initial part of the section.
